headline: Citi Cuts Palantir Price Target to $210 from $260, Keeps Buy Rating
summary: Citi analyst lowered Palantir price target citing multiple compression in software sector. PLTR down ~22% YTD despite strong AI demand fundamentals. Q1 2026 earnings due May 4.

headline: Software Sector Sell-Off: PLTR, SNOW, CRM Down 20-33% YTD Despite Strong Fundamentals
summary: Broad AI software sell-off driven by valuation concerns and AI disruption fears rather than weak fundamentals. Snowflake signed largest deal in company history ($400M+ TCV) and seven $100M+ deals in Q4.

headline: UBS Downgrades ServiceNow to Neutral, Cuts Target to $100 from $170
summary: UBS analyst Karl Keirstead questions whether ServiceNow competitive position is resilient to AI disruption. Significant target cut reflects cautious view on enterprise software.

headline: OpenAI Poaches Enterprise Sales Stars from Salesforce, Snowflake, and Palantir
summary: OpenAI aggressively hiring enterprise sales talent from established AI software companies, signaling intensifying competition in enterprise AI market.

headline: Hyperscaler Capex Surges to $630B+ for 2026, Up 62% YoY
summary: Big Five hyperscalers plan record capex: Amazon $200B, Google $175-185B, Meta $115-135B, Microsoft $110-120B, Oracle $50B. ~75% ($450B) tied directly to AI infrastructure. Markets remain supply-constrained.

headline: JPMorgan Downgrades Meta to Neutral After Q1 Earnings, Cuts Target to $725
summary: Meta raised 2026 AI capex outlook sharply. JPMorgan analyst expects capex to hit $202B by 2027, pushing Meta into negative FCF (~-$4B in 2026, -$24B in 2027). Stock fell 10%+ post-earnings.

headline: Alphabet Q1: Cloud Revenue Jumps 63% YoY to $20B, Market Cap Passes $4.5T
summary: Google Cloud cleared $20B single-quarter revenue for first time. Growth accelerated from 34% to 48% to 63% over past three quarters. Shares jumped ~9%. Strong AI demand validation.

headline: Meta Nuclear Deals: 6.6 GW Secured from Vistra, TerraPower, Oklo, Constellation
summary: Meta becomes one of largest corporate nuclear energy purchasers in US history. Deals include Vistra Perry/Davis-Besse/Beaver Valley plants, TerraPower advanced reactors by 2032, Oklo campus by 2030, and Constellation Clinton plant by 2027.

headline: Constellation Energy Fair Value Cut 7.3% by Analysts Despite 40% YTD Gain
summary: Mixed analyst sentiment on CEG. BNP Paribas initiates with Outperform, but guidance disappointments and target reductions reflect cautious views on nuclear capacity expansion timeline.

headline: Vistra Strong Buy Consensus: 19 Buy Ratings, Average Target $234 (52% Upside)
summary: VST trading at ~$155. Analyst consensus remains Strong Buy with price targets ranging $97-$318. Nuclear fleet powering AI datacenter demand is key growth driver.

headline: SMCI Expands Building Block Solutions with Arm-Based Platforms and OCP Systems
summary: Super Micro targeting 6,000 racks/month production capacity including 3,000 liquid-cooled racks by end of fiscal 2026. New Arm AGI CPU servers and OCP ORv3-compliant rack offerings.

headline: Vertiv Developing 800 VDC Power Portfolio Aligned with NVIDIA Roadmap for H2 2026
summary: Vertiv continues to see record orders as liquid cooling adoption accelerates. New 800V DC power and cooling portfolio aligned with NVIDIA upcoming GPU generations, expected H2 2026 release.

headline: RBC Raises Celestica Target to $440 from $400, Maintains Outperform
summary: RBC Capital analyst Paul Treiber raises CLS price target on continued strong datacenter hardware demand. 13 analysts have Buy consensus on Celestica.

headline: Dell AI Server Backlog Reaches $18.4B with $5.6B Shipped in Q3 FY2026
summary: Dell integrating liquid cooling directly into supply chain, offering racks capable of 480kW power — nearly 20x increase from standards two years ago. Strong AI infrastructure positioning.

headline: TSMC Q1 2026: Profit Jumps 58%, Fourth Consecutive Record Quarter
summary: Revenue up 40.6% YoY with HPC (AI/5G) at 61% of revenue. Advanced nodes ~75% of wafer revenue. Capex guidance $52-56B for 2026 (up from $40.5B). Q2 revenue forecast $39-40.2B.

headline: ASML Raises 2026 Revenue Forecast to €36-40B, Plans 25% More EUV Tool Shipments
summary: ASML Q1 sales €8.8B, net income €2.8B. Raised full-year outlook confirms AI chip demand accelerating not cooling. Chipmakers placing heavy orders to keep up.

headline: NVIDIA Locks Up Majority of TSMC Advanced Packaging (CoWoS) Capacity
summary: Advanced packaging emerging as next bottleneck for AI chips. TSMC outsourcing some steps to ASE, whose advanced packaging sales are doubling in 2026. Two new packaging facilities planned in Arizona.

headline: Broadcom AI Semiconductor Revenue Projected to Exceed $30B in FY2026
summary: Goldman Sachs analyst rates AVGO as critical AI infrastructure provider with $450 price target. Strong Buy consensus from 30 analysts with average target $443.
